codeReview.summary: The follow-up commit correctly removes the hardcoded `lng: "zh"` path and fixes the previously flagged toasts, confirms, and aria labels in the reviewed pages, but the promised translation audit is still incomplete because several user-facing accessibility labels remain hardcoded in English outside the locale files.

issues.description: The feature claims to move all remaining user-facing strings behind `t()`, but multiple accessibility labels are still hardcoded in English: `aria-label="Select platforms"` in `src/components/central/InstallDialog.tsx:121` and `src/components/collection/CollectionInstallDialog.tsx:108`, `aria-label="Select skills"` in `src/components/collection/SkillPickerDialog.tsx:137`, `aria-label="Select collections"` in `src/components/collection/CollectionPickerDialog.tsx:108`, and `Close` in `src/components/ui/dialog.tsx:127`. Chinese mode will still expose English screen-reader copy, so the translation-coverage blocker from `ui-i18n-setup` is not fully resolved.

The glossary normalization is still incomplete. `settings.scanDirsDesc` and `settings.customPlatformsDesc` keep the English noun `skills` (`src/i18n/locales/zh.json:186` and `:197`) instead of using the agreed Chinese terminology, so the locale is still slightly inconsistent.

summary: Fail. The persistence bug is fixed, but the re-review still finds untranslated accessibility labels and dialog-close copy outside the locale resources, so the complete i18n-coverage requirement remains unmet.
